This is a simple Java server that runs a CoreNLP pipeline over a socket or stdin/named pipe.  It's useful for running CoreNLP over a network connection, or for running CoreNLP over a named pipe for batch processing.

Download the latest version of CoreNLP from [here](http://stanfordnlp.github.io/CoreNLP/download.html).  Put the jar files in the `lib` directory.  Download the latest version of Stanford CoreNLP from [here](http://stanfordnlp.github.io/CoreNLP/download.html).  Put the jar files in the `stanford-corenlp-full-2015-04-20` directory.  Download the latest version of the CoreNLP Java Server from [here](https://github.com/stanfordnlp/CoreNLP-JavaServer/releases).  Put the jar file in the `stanford-corenlp-full-2015-04-20` directory.

The server can be configured with a CoreNLP configuration file or a JSON dictionary of properties.  The configuration file is used if the `--configfile` option is specified, otherwise the JSON dictionary is used.  The JSON dictionary is used if the `--configdict` option is specified.
